steps.title: Fastest path: find the rate per second
steps.content: Divide the amount of water in one drop by the number of seconds per drop. Then use the initial volume as the constant term in a linear model.

steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The container starts at 18 mL. One 0.08 mL drop falls every 8 seconds, so the rate is $\frac{0.08}{8}=0.01$ mL per second. The question asks which equation models $v$ (total volume) after $t$ seconds.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — $v=8t$: ignores the starting 18 mL and uses the wrong rate. ✗
  • Choice B — $v=0.64t+18$: rate 0.64 mL/s would mean 0.64×8=5.12 mL per drop, far too fast. ✗
  • Choice C — $v=0.01t+18$: starts at 18 mL and adds 0.01 mL each second, matching the calculated rate. ✓
  • Choice D — $v=0.08t+18$: uses 0.08 mL/s instead of 0.08 mL per 8 s — off by a factor of 8. ✗
That confirms C is correct.
